headline: MCP is now supported natively by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and major IDEs — the N×M integration era is ending.
summary: Model Context Protocol, launched by Anthropic in Nov 2024, has been adopted by every major LLM vendor and coding IDE within 8 months. The ecosystem now has 500+ community servers plus first-party servers from Microsoft (Azure), GitHub, Cloudflare, and others. This makes MCP the de-facto standard for LLM↔tool integration.
why_it_matters: Any OKF or knowledge platform not exposed as MCP by end of 2025 is architecturally behind. This is the leverage point of the decade for the twin platform.
action: Add 'OKF-MCP server v0' to your GitHub project board this week; scaffold with the official Python SDK (`pip install mcp`).

headline: Microsoft GraphRAG adds DRIFT search and lazy indexing — cost per corpus drops ~40%.
summary: Recent GraphRAG releases introduced DRIFT (dynamic community traversal) for hybrid local+global queries and lazy indexing that defers community-summary generation until queried. Combined, they cut typical indexing cost dramatically while preserving multi-hop quality. Neo4j and LlamaIndex have shipped compatible implementations.
why_it_matters: Removes the biggest complaint about GraphRAG (upfront indexing cost). Your twin can now afford GraphRAG from day one, not phase two.
action: Re-run your ontology draft through GraphRAG with lazy mode enabled; benchmark cost delta.

headline: n8n's AI Agent node now includes native MCP client support and streaming responses.
summary: n8n's LangChain-based AI Agent node has added first-class MCP client capabilities, letting workflows call any MCP server as a tool without custom code. Streaming outputs mean long-running twin queries can push partial results into downstream nodes.
why_it_matters: Your existing n8n workflow can now consume your future OKF-MCP server directly — no bespoke HTTP glue.
action: Update n8n to latest; add one AI Agent node pointed at a public MCP server for smoke testing.
